name: Saptamatrikas
description: The Saptamatrikas, also known as the Seven Mothers, are a group of powerful goddesses in the Hindu pantheon. They are considered to be personifications of the energy (Shakti) of different gods. The most commonly recognized Matrikas are Brahmani (Shakti of Brahma), Maheshwari (Shakti of Shiva), Kaumari (Shakti of Kartikeya), Vaishnavi (Shakti of Vishnu), Varahi (Shakti of Varaha, Vishnu's boar avatar), Indrani (Shakti of Indra), and Chamunda (Shakti of Devi). Their origins are debated, with some texts linking them to Skanda (Kartikeya) and others to Devi. They are often associated with war and protection, particularly of children, and are invoked for fertility and prosperity. Each Matrika is depicted with specific attributes and mounts mirroring those of their corresponding male deities. They are worshipped individually and collectively, especially in rural areas, and are considered fierce protectors against evil. Their iconography can vary, sometimes appearing benevolent and other times terrifying, reflecting their dual nature as both nurturing mothers and fierce warriors. They are integral to Shaktism and Tantric traditions, often depicted alongside Shiva and other major deities.
subtitle: The Seven Divine Mothers of Power

keyMyths.title: Origin from Devi
keyMyths.description: In some traditions, the Saptamatrikas arise from Devi's anger to combat demons. They are fierce warriors born from her divine energy.

keyMyths.title: Association with Skanda
keyMyths.description: The Matrikas are sometimes considered the mothers or nurses of Skanda (Kartikeya), the god of war. They nurture and protect him.

keyMyths.title: Battle Against Andhakasura
keyMyths.description: The Saptamatrikas assist Devi in her battle against the demon Andhakasura, drinking his blood to prevent it from creating more demons.
